weather.summary: Saturday: 16°C, overcast clouds. Sunday: 9°C, light rain

metadata.greeting: This weekend in Hackney is bursting with fun for your little one! Get ready for a weekend of adventure with farm animals, creative workshops, and exciting explorations at museums. Don't forget your wellies for Sunday, and maybe a sun hat for Saturday!

activities.title: Vauxhall City Farm Easter Activities
activities.description: Enjoy free Easter hunts and bonnet-making crafts at Vauxhall City Farm, meet goats, sheep and smaller animals.
activities.whyItWorks: A great chance to see animals and engage in creative activities close to home. Perfect for a 3-year-old's love for animals and crafting.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground line to Highbury & Islington station, then the Victoria line to Vauxhall station. Then walk to the farm.
transport.tip: Check the weather before heading out, as some activities are weather-dependent.

activities.title: Library Club at the Hackney Showroom
activities.description: Reading corner and diverse book-pairing service.
activities.whyItWorks: A relaxed and engaging space for kids to explore books and stories, fostering a love for reading from a young age.

transport.fromHackney: Short bus ride or walk from central Hackney.
transport.tip: Check term times before heading out.

activities.title: Inside Aardman: Wallace & Gromit and Friends at Young V&A
activities.address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, London E2 9PA
activities.description: Explore how the iconic characters are brought to life.
activities.whyItWorks: The Young V&A offers lots of hands-on activities and the exhibit gives unlimited visits.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central line to Bethnal Green station.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.description: Two floors of immersive play space known as the Story Worlds as well as the Story Garden playground. The museum hosts Baby Storytelling plus crafts and singalong time for babies and toddlers.
activities.whyItWorks: Immersive play spaces and regular storytelling sessions are ideal for engaging a 3-year-old's imagination and providing active fun.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground line to Stratford station.
transport.tip: Check the schedule for specific activities suitable for toddlers.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: A small and beautifully designed wooden playground with a sweet pond theme.
activities.whyItWorks: This playground offers a safe and engaging space for toddlers to explore and play, with a charming theme that sparks their imagination.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground line to Stratford station.
transport.tip: Perfect for a short play session after visiting the Discover Children's Story Centre.

activities.title: Museum of the Home
activities.description: Step into historical homes through the ages, with hands-on activities and an enclosed garden.
activities.whyItWorks: Exploration and activity sheets available for kids.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus or Overground line to Hoxton station.
transport.tip: Pick up an activity sheet or explorer backpack from the front desk.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: A community farm with a variety of animals and gardens.
activities.whyItWorks: Perfect for animal lovers! A great opportunity to see and learn about farm animals in an urban setting.

transport.fromHackney: Located in Hackney, easily accessible by bus or walking.
transport.tip: Check the farm's website for special events and feeding times.

activities.title: Free Easter Weekender at Camden Market
activities.description: Easter art trail, face painting and bonnet making.
activities.whyItWorks: Provides a variety of sensory experiences for a 3-year-old, including art, crafts, and the vibrant atmosphere of Camden Market.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Camden Road station, or the Northern Line to Camden Town station.
transport.tip: Bring cash for activities and food stalls.

activities.title: Natural History Museum Easter Family Festival
activities.address: Cromwell Road, South Kensington, London SW7 5BD
activities.description: Learn from scientists and a person-sized T. Rex. LEGO Super Builder sessions, family talks and workshops.
activities.whyItWorks: Dinosaurs, hands-on exhibits, and interactive workshops make it a thrilling and educational experience for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground line to Highbury & Islington station, then the Piccadilly line to South Kensington station.
transport.tip: Arrive early to avoid long queues, especially during the Easter holidays.

activities.title: Science Museum: Destination Space (Easter Programme)
activities.address: Exhibition Road, South Kensington, London SW7 2DD
activities.description: Explore the wonders of space with hands-on exhibits and activities suitable for all ages.
activities.whyItWorks: The Garden in the basement (open Tues – Sunday) is an interactive space where children can discover construction, water, light and sound through play. The Pattern Pod is great for light exploration, sensory and interactive fun.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground line to Highbury & Islington station, then the Piccadilly line to South Kensington station.
transport.tip: Check for specific toddler-friendly activities and shows on the day of your visit.

activities.title: Cutty Sark Easter Egg Trail
activities.price: Paid entry to the ship required
activities.description: Follow a free family trail and discover what life was like on board as a 19th-century sailor and join in musical storytelling sessions inspired by Cutty Sark's crew.
activities.whyItWorks: Great for energetic toddlers due to space to roam.

transport.fromHackney: Take the DLR directly to Cutty Sark station.
transport.tip: Check the Cutty Sark website for specific event times and booking information.

activities.title: Easter Bunny Experience at Mudchute
activities.description: Meet the Easter Bunny and get up close with farm animals, including goats, lambs, and rabbits. Visit the Bunny's house, collect an Easter egg, and feed the animals!
activities.whyItWorks: Meeting the Easter Bunny and interacting with animals is always a hit with toddlers. The short sessions are perfect for their attention spans.

transport.fromHackney: Take the DLR to Mudchute station.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance as sessions are limited.

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: A small zoo in Hampstead Heath with rare species of birds and animals, including wallabies, lemurs, and Scottish wildcats.
activities.whyItWorks: Free entry and a manageable size for toddlers make it an easy and enjoyable outing for seeing animals.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground line to Gospel Oak station, then the Overground line to Golders Green station.
transport.tip: Combine your visit with a picnic in the park.

activities.title: Diana Memorial Playground
activities.description: A large playground with a Peter Pan-inspired pirate ship, sensory trail, teepees, and plenty of seating for parents.
activities.whyItWorks: The pirate ship and sensory trail provide plenty of opportunities for imaginative play and exploration.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central line to Notting Hill Gate station, then walk through Kensington Gardens.
transport.tip: The playground can get crowded, so arriving early is recommended.

activities.title: Hall Place & Gardens – Easter Celebrations
activities.description: Explore Hall Place's historic house and gardens, with Easter egg hunts, trails, and workshops. Seasonal blooms and family-friendly events create an engaging day out.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines history, nature, and Easter-themed activities, offering a diverse and engaging experience for toddlers and their families.

transport.fromHackney: Take a train from London Bridge to Bexley station, then a short bus ride to Hall Place.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance for Easter egg hunts and workshops.

activities.title: Polka Theatre - Laaaunch!
activities.description: The multi-sensory space-themed Laaaunch!
activities.whyItWorks: It's multi sensory and space themed.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Wimbledon.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance

activities.title: Unicorn Theatre - Planet Omar
activities.description: A show at the Unicorn Theatre.
activities.whyItWorks: The Unicorn Theatre usually has great shows for kids.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance

activities.title: Southbank Centre - REPLAY: A Limitless Recycled Playground
activities.description: Explore a world of play and invention in a recycled playground for children and their grown-ups.
activities.whyItWorks: This playground is made out of repurposed materials.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Waterloo.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance

activities.title: Southbank Centre - WordPlay: Ages 3 – 5
activities.description: Songs sessions for toddlers
activities.whyItWorks: Fun for families.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Waterloo.
transport.tip: Check the schedule for specific activities suitable for toddlers.

activities.title: Kew Gardens - Bluey at Kew
activities.price: Included with entry to the gardens.
activities.description: Bluey-themed interactive activities for the whole family, including a Play Zone with 3D installations and the chance to meet the Bluey character.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive and great for fans of Bluey.

transport.fromHackney: Tube and train to Kew Gardens.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Hampton Court Palace Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt
activities.price: Included with entry to the palace
activities.description: Hop through stunning palace gardens to find 10 gold bunny statues! Match historical names to their ribbons to solve the puzzle and win a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: Egg hunts are always a hit.

transport.fromHackney: Train to Hampton Court.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Hobbledown Heath Easter Eggsplorers
activities.description: Follow the themed egg trail, climb and explore across indoor and outdoor play, and meet animals along the way.
activities.whyItWorks: The egg trail, indoor playbarn, and animal encounters are great for a 3 year old.

transport.fromHackney: Tube and train to Hounslow.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: London Zoo Zoonormous Egg Hunt
activities.description: Wander past giraffes, lions, monkeys and more incredible wildlife as you search for hidden eggs on a special Easter egg hunt
activities.whyItWorks: Animals plus an egg hunt!

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Regent's Park
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.
